name: Salvage Beetle
oracle_text: Reach\nWhenever an artifact you control is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, put a +1/+1 counter on ~.
flavor_text: "The ruins feed the land. The land feeds us. The cycle continues." — Sura, Rendon Ranchmaster
art_prompt: A stocky chitinous beetle with iridescent green-bronze carapace crawls across weathered metal debris. Its segmented legs grip rusted gears and broken machinery. Long spined antennae curve upward, and a faint metallic sheen clings to its armored body, suggesting it feeds on industrial remnants in a reclaimed wasteland.. Stylized digital illustration, wide naturalist composition, rich earth tones with dappled light, layered depth, post-apocalyptic science-fantasy setting.
design_notes: This card is a green uncommon artifact creature at CMC 2 with straightforward mechanical identity. Reach is an evergreen keyword fitting green's fight-and-control strategy. The triggered ability ties into the set's artifact-matters theme by rewarding artifact sacrifice or destruction — a clean interaction with cards like Spark Detonator, Reclaim the Surface, and artifact-based sacrifice loops. The ability creates real variability (actual artifact deaths in-game), respecting the "no fake variability" rule. P+T of 2/2 at CMC 2 is on-curve for green, and the +1/+1 scaling potential makes it a relevant draft pick without being dominant. No keyword nonbos: reach + 2/2 body + triggered growth is cohesive. The Insect Beast creature type reinforces the post-apocalyptic nature setting and fits the renewal theme of the flavor text.
